title: Search Optimization: Pagination Cache
impact: 90%+ faster for paginated result navigation; Improved UX for large result sets

tasks.description: Define PaginatedCacheEntry Interface - Create interface with fullResults array, totalCount number, and timestamp number for TTL tracking
acceptanceCriteria: PaginatedCacheEntry<T> generic interface defined
Contains fullResults: T[], totalCount: number, timestamp: number
Interface supports any result type (Entity, SearchResult, etc.)

tasks.description: Implement Pagination Cache in SearchManager - Add paginationCache Map with 30-second TTL constant, implement buildSearchCacheKey() for unique query identification, store full results on first page request
testCategories: Different queries have different cache keys
acceptanceCriteria: paginationCache: Map<string, PaginatedCacheEntry<Entity>> property added
PAGINATION_CACHE_TTL = 30_000 constant defined
buildSearchCacheKey() creates unique key from query + filters
First page request stores full results in cache
Subsequent page requests slice cached results
Cache entry includes timestamp for TTL checking

tasks.description: Integrate Pagination Cache with Search Methods - Modify searchNodes(), searchNodesRanked(), booleanSearch(), fuzzySearch() to use pagination cache for offset/limit operations, return PaginatedResult with totalCount and hasMore
acceptanceCriteria: All search methods check pagination cache before executing
Cache key includes method name to avoid collisions
PaginatedResult type returned with results, totalCount, hasMore
Backward compatibility maintained for non-paginated calls
Cache hit slices from fullResults without re-executing search

tasks.description: Add Cache Cleanup - Implement periodic cleanup of expired entries, add invalidatePaginationCache() for manual clearing after mutations, integrate with entity mutation hooks
acceptanceCriteria: Expired entries removed on cache access
invalidatePaginationCache() method implemented
Cache cleared after entity create/update/delete
No memory leaks from stale cache entries

tasks.description: Add Pagination Cache Tests - Test cache behavior across page navigation, TTL expiration, cache invalidation, and performance improvements
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies first page stores results
Test verifies second page is <5ms
Test verifies TTL expiration after 30 seconds
Test verifies cache invalidation on mutation
Test verifies PaginatedResult structure
8+ new tests added

successCriteria: Pagination cache stores full results on first page request
Subsequent page requests use cached results (90%+ faster)
PaginatedResult type with totalCount and hasMore
notes: This sprint completes the Search Optimization phase. Pagination is a common pattern where users browse through result pages. Without caching, each page request re-executes the full search and discards most results. With caching, the full result set is computed once and pages are served from cache. The 30-second TTL is chosen to cover typical browsing sessions while ensuring data freshness. This sprint is independent of other search cache sprints (3-4) and can be developed in parallel.
